steps.title: Understand the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The previous sentence gives one reason Houston Black is a fitting state soil: it is abundant across Texas. The blank introduces a second sentence giving another reason: it plays an important agricultural role. Both sentences support the same conclusion. The blank must signal that a second supporting reason is being added, not a contrast, alternative, or time reference.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Additionally,': Signals that a second point is being added to support the same conclusion — exactly right. Both sentences give reasons Houston Black is a fitting choice. ✓
  • Choice B — 'At that time,': Signals a time reference, implying the agricultural role existed at some earlier moment described in the passage. No past time period is referenced; the sentence is about the soil's present role. ✗
  • Choice C — 'By contrast,': Signals a contradiction or opposing point. The agricultural role does not contradict abundance across the state — both support the same claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Alternatively,': Signals a different option or substitute. The agricultural role is not an alternative to the abundance point; both reasons coexist. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
